title: Theory of Computation
description: Practice year-wise MCQs from 2018 to 2026 with explanations and answer review.

questions.prompt: A pushdown automaton is more powerful than a finite automaton because it has:
questions.explanation: The stack allows a PDA to remember unbounded information.
A Turing machine is a general model of computation.

questions.prompt: The set of all strings that are not accepted by a Turing machine is:
questions.explanation: The halting problem shows that some properties of Turing machines are undecidable.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is true for regular languages?
options: They are not closed under union
They are not closed under concatenation
They cannot be recognized by automata
questions.explanation: Regular languages are closed under union, intersection, concatenation, and complementation.

questions.prompt: A nondeterministic finite automaton can be converted to:
questions.explanation: Every NFA has an equivalent DFA.

questions.prompt: Which class of languages is accepted by a linear-bounded automaton?
The pumping lemma is used to show that a language is:
questions.explanation: The pumping lemma helps prove that certain languages are not regular or not context-free.
